author: piyooko
description: Seamless spaced repetition, flashcards, and incremental reading workflows. A stylized, modern interface alternative to Anki for your plain-text knowledge base. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: piyooko
manifest.description: Seamless spaced repetition, flashcards, and incremental reading workflows. A stylized, modern interface alternative to Anki for your plain-text knowledge base.
manifest.name: Syro

name: Syro

releases.author: piyooko
releases.description:
### New Features
-   **Incremental Reading Extracts**: Introduced a new extract module. Combine it with [Epub Importer](https://github.com/aoout/obsidian-epub-importer) or [MinerU](https://mineru.net/OpenSourceTools/Extractor) to set up a SuperMemo-like incremental reading flow in Obsidian.
-   **Create Extract Command**: Select text in the editor and use a custom shortcut to quickly wrap it as an extract.
-   **Smart Full-Text Slicing**: Auto-generate an extract queue for an entire note based on headings via the file menu or title. This does not change your original Markdown text.
-   **Memos & Hover Tooltips**: Add notes and thoughts to your extracts directly in the editor or during reviews.
-   **Priority Weighting**: Added a `W: 1-10` weight control on review cards. Quickly adjust how soon an extract shows up in your review queue.
-   **New Deck Review Modes**: Added daily limits for new and reviewed extracts in deck settings. Now supports extract-first, flashcard-first, and mixed review modes.
-   **Review Edit Shortcut**: Press `Alt+E` to edit extracts directly during a review. This makes it easy to create cards or highlight text while reading.
-   **Expanded Timeline Support**: The timeline now shows standard, auto-generated, and graduated extracts. You can also edit or clear memos right from the sidebar.
-   **Timeline Limit Removed**: The previous 10-item limit is gone. You can now save unlimited reading progress, extracts, and thoughts for a single file.
-   **Experimental Multi-Device Sync**: Added basic sync for extracts. **If you have sync problems, try copying data from your most updated device to the outdated one.** Please also submit an [Issue](https://github.com/piyooko/obsidian-syro/issues) with steps to reproduce the bug.

### Improvements
-   **Unified Deck Entry**: The file header entry now uses the same logic as the Deck Tree. This makes deck card counts consistent everywhere.
-   **Due Date UI Improvements**: Improved the custom due date menu in the review screen. Default days, button text, and clicks are now clearer and easier to use.

releases.author: piyooko
releases.description:
> ## **Important Notice**
> This release includes a major upgrade to Syro's underlying user data structure. Syro will automatically save a backup of your previous data in `migration-backups` during migration, but we still strongly recommend manually backing up your Syro data before upgrading.

### New Features

-   Session-Based Multi-Device Sync: Introduced a new Syro sync architecture for cards, notes, Timeline history, and deck options, with protected migration from the previous data layout.
-   Device Management & Recovery: Added a dedicated Sync settings area for selecting the current device, rebuilding from another device, pulling another device snapshot, renaming devices, and cleaning up invalid device folders.
-   Inline Note Review Button: Added an inline review control beside note titles in source mode, showing the note's reviewable and total card counts with quick access to note-level review actions.

### Improvements

-   Settings Reorganization: Added dedicated Parsing and Sync tabs and regrouped related options into clearer sections.
-   Safer Data Persistence: Split Syro data into more focused device-scoped files and improved protection for in-progress changes during review and sync flows.
-   Reduced File Churn: Reduced unnecessary file writes during review sessions and limited routine persistence to the state that actually needs to change.
-   Mobile & Sync UI Polish: Refined the mobile review chrome, improved device card layout and tooltips, and polished the Sync settings interface for better readability.
-   Stronger Regression Coverage: Added broader automated coverage for migration, startup recovery, multi-device review flows, and session replay.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features

-   Timeline for Untracked Notes: Added an experimental toggle allowing you to use the Timeline sidebar on regular, untracked Markdown notes.
-   Timeline Auto-Follow: Added an experimental toggle that makes the Timeline automatically switch to and display the source note of the card you are currently reviewing.
-   Cram Mode Command: Introduced a new specific command to "Review cards in this note" using Cram Mode.

### Improvements

-   Auto-Tracking via Duration Prefix: When working in untracked notes, manually typing a duration prefix (e.g., `2d::`) will now automatically add the note to your tracked items and schedule a review.
-   Expanded UI Customization: Added new options in Style Settings to enable square corners for the review area and sidebar. Adjusted group title spacing in settings for better readability.
-   Streamlined Card Creation: Removed redundant popups and overlays from the top right corner during card creation for a cleaner, distraction-free experience.
-   General UI Polish: Optimized card scrolling positioning, answer expansion behaviors, and various interaction details across the deck tree and review sessions.

### Bug Fixes

-   Fixed an issue where Anki-style clozes were incorrectly grouped and generated when placed inside Markdown tables or lists.
-   Fixed incorrect rendering of cloze cards caused by mixed or cross-closed text formatting.
-   Fixed the Timeline sidebar's auto-follow logic and tab-focus behavior when switching between different files.
-   Fixed the standard "Review cards in this note" command and resolved related review session switching bugs.
-   Fixed the English localization display for the deck options preset usage count.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features

-   Desktop & Mobile Style Settings: Independent UI styling groups for desktop and mobile, allowing granular customization of deck trees, card reviews, sidebars, and timelines.
-   Device-Aware Menus: Style Settings now automatically filter to show only the options relevant to your current device.
-   One-Click Resets: Added reset buttons to parent groups in Style Settings to instantly restore entire sections to default.

### Improvements

-   UI Polish: Refined Style Settings layout, spacing, and wording (e.g., renamed "Review" to "Card Review").
-   Resizable Timeline: You can now drag the expanded timeline header to adjust the panel height.
-   Cleaner Timelines: Removed empty message rows for timeline entries that only contain time/pills.

### Bug Fixes

-   Fixed manual timeline prefixes (like `2d::`) failing to reschedule review times.
-   Fixed single-click collapse behavior on the timeline header.
-   Fixed CodeMirror `Selection points outside of document` errors during timeline text updates.
-   Fixed Anki-style cloze rendering so same-ID clozes are strictly limited to the current line.
-   Improved parsing and review stability for mixed curly-brace cloze formats.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Mobile UI Rollout and Scheduling Cleanup
#### Highlights

* Added broad mobile UI support across the plugin.
* Reworked the settings experience for phones and touch devices.
* Improved the note review sidebar and timeline behavior inside mobile drawers.
* Adjusted review layouts, deck tree behavior, and modal sizing for smaller screens.
* Removed the desktop-only manifest restriction.
* Aligned flashcard scheduling semantics more closely with FSRS and simplified legacy scheduling paths.
* Added regression tests for the new mobile review view and React settings mount flow.

---

#### What’s New

* **Syro now officially supports mobile**, removing the previous desktop-only limitation.
* The settings UI now has dedicated mobile adaptations, including better spacing, layout behavior, and touch-friendly controls.
* The note review sidebar has been reworked for mobile drawers, including improved timeline behavior and phone-specific sizing rules.
* Flashcard and review layouts were updated to behave more naturally on narrow screens.
* Deck tree resizing and panel behavior were adjusted for phones and tablets.
* Several mobile-specific UI issues were fixed, including switch compression, color picker alignment, and settings control spacing.

---

#### Under the Hood

* Flashcards now clearly follow FSRS, while note review continues to use the built-in WMS scheduler.
* Short-term scheduling terminology and compatibility behavior were aligned with FSRS semantics, with silent migration for existing data.
* Legacy scheduling branches were cleaned up to reduce complexity and improve long-term maintainability.
* The build pipeline was updated to better support the React-based mobile UI, including improved handling for framer-motion.
* Added parser compatibility improvements for Anki-style cloze content in edge cases.

---

### Stability and Testing

* Added new tests covering the mobile note review view behavior.
* Added tests for the React settings root mounting flow.
* Expanded regression coverage around the note review sidebar and scheduling/settings compatibility.

---

### Docs

* Updated documentation to clarify that flashcards use FSRS and note review uses WMS.
* Refined note review wording to describe WMS as the built-in scheduler.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features

-   Folder Tracking & Dynamic Tags: You can now set up auto-tracking for specific folders. You also have the option to set up rules that automatically bulk-apply specific tags to both existing and newly created markdown notes within those folders.
-   Sidebar Progress Toggle: Added a new switch in the settings so you can independently show or hide the reading progress indicator in the review sidebar.
-   File Path Tooltips: Hovering over a note in the sidebar can now display its relative path within your vault. You can toggle this feature on or off and customize the hover delay in your settings.

### Improvements

-   Smoother Folder Tracking UI: Polished the folder tracking menu with better text, improved spacing, and a more intuitive workflow for setting up auto-tags.
-   Refined "Safe Truncation" Settings: The settings panel for handling long-context truncation is now much clearer and more reliable to configure.
-   Cleaner Settings Menu: Removed the overly technical object-level debug tracking. This removes unnecessary clutter from your settings panel and keeps the plugin running lean.
-   Better Documentation: Updated and reorganized both the English and Chinese README files so the plugin's structure and instructions are much easier to follow.

### Bug Fixes

-   Tracking Logic Priority: Fixed an issue with note tracking resolution. Manual tracking will now reliably override folder-based rules, and legacy tag data will no longer interfere with your tracking results.
-   Tooltip Behavior: Fixed the sidebar file path tooltips so they correctly respect your custom hover delay settings before appearing.
-   Folder Tracking UI Tweaks: Squashed several minor visual bugs in the folder tracking menu, including incorrect button text, weird spacing, and minor interaction glitches.
-   Text & Display Consistency: Fixed various text inconsistencies between the sidebar and timeline, including keeping timeline titles in English and removing leftover internal placeholder text from the auto-tag button.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
-   Kept progress bar styling in settings while making deck options the only visibility toggle for review countdown bars.
-   Wired the review countdown bar to respect the configured color, warning color, height, and fill direction.
-   Released version `0.0.7` with aligned manifest, package, compatibility metadata, and release notes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
-   Restored the async review sequencer contract so review actions no longer break the TypeScript test pipeline after the review-flow refactor.
-   Released version `0.0.6` with aligned manifest, package, and compatibility metadata for distribution.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixed the sync progress toast so runtime status text uses locale keys instead of corrupted hard-coded strings.
- Fixed the review session first-card render path so the front side appears on the initial screen without reopening or switching cards.
- Added a reviewer-aligned mojibake audit script and kept the release repository file delta limited to the intended tracked additions.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Removed the default Anki-cloze hotkeys from the shipped command metadata for community review compliance.
- Replaced the custom folder suggester, legacy trash call, `localStorage` popover sizing, `navigator.platform`, and `new Function` interpolation with review-safe implementations.
- Moved status bar due styling out of runtime style injection and into static CSS-driven presentation for the public release build.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
